title: Mission to Mos Eisley (Galactic Civil War)

{{Quote|Chances were, the captured crew were either dead or sold as slaves already, but no one in Renegade Squadron believed in abandoning good men.|Col Serra speaks of the battle years later|<ref name="fug" />}}
Around [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] captured a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] members that included [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]]. Fett delivered Ackbar to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and sold [[Gender/Legends|his]] remaining crew into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] to the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]] in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. The Alliance responded by assigning its new elite fighting force, [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]], created by [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]]-turned-Rebels [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Col Serra]], on a '''mission''' to rescue the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]]. The local Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] on Tatooine attempted to stop the Rebels from freeing their comrades, but Renegade Squadron rescued the prisoners. The Rebels initially believed the mission to be a success and a miraculous rescue effort on the part of Renegade Squadron.

However, Fett had placed a [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracking device]] in an [[Unidentified R5-series astromech droid|R5-series astromech droid]] that had accompanied Renegade Squadron on the mission, leading the Imperials to the Rebels' new base on [[Boz Pity/Legends|Boz Pity]]. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, following the Empire's defeat and the [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|formation]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Serra recounted Renegade Squadron's Mos Eisley rescue mission to [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Tionne Solusar]] for her [[Jedi Archives/Legends|archives]].

==Prelude==
[[File:MosEisley-BFRS.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Boba Fett's ''Slave I'' lands on Tatooine.]]
During the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] around [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] sent [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] to [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]] on a [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] mission to determine if the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was fit to host a new Alliance base after the loss of their [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. However, before he could reach Saleucami, Ackabar's [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|transport]] was [[Skirmish over Saleucami|attacked]] by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who was looking to collect the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on captured Rebels put up by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Fett disabled Ackbar's transport over the planet by firing [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] from his [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']], upon the transport's [[Engine/Legends|engines]], thereby destroying them. The attack [[Death/Legends|killed]] some of Ackbar's crew, and Fett was able to capture the Mon Calamari admiral and his remaining comrades.<ref name="fug" />

Soon after, Fett sold Ackbar to the Galactic Empire, and when the Rebels liberated the Admiral in a [[Battle of Kessel (0 ABY)|battle at Kessel]], he revealed that the crew had been sold into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] to the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]] in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] [[City/Legends|city]] located on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] dispatched the Rebellion's new unit, [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]]—a group composed of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] formed and led by former [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Col Serra]]—to Tatooine in hopes of rescuing the [[Prison/Legends|prisoners]]. They were unaware that the entire scenario was actually a trap orchestrated by the Empire.<ref name="fug" />

==The mission==
Renegade Squadron arrived at Mos Eisley aboard Solo's ship, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], and immediately deployed on the southern side of the city. The [[Squadron/Legends|squadron's]] second-in-command, Col Serra, ordered the Renegades to go and capture the local [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] to see if anyone there would reveal information for the price of a few [[Alcohol/Legends|drinks]], and to get a stronger foothold in the city. While en route to the cantina, they were ambushed by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] from the local Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]].<ref name="fug" />

[[File:PIE_3.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Imperial garrison deploys their stormtroopers.]]
After fighting their way through the stormtroopers, Renegade Squadron arrived at the cantina and fought off the stormtroopers stationed there. Once they had successfully captured the building, the squadron found a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] with [[Unidentified Rodian contact|Rebel sympathies]], who agreed to take them to where the prisoners were being held. The Renegades followed the Rodian across the street to a [[Deflector shield/Legends|force field]] protected [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] owned by the Hutts, fending off additional Imperials who were trying to stop the Rodian. The Rodian identified that the hangar was were the prisoners were being held, so Solo dispatched the squadron to find and destroy the three [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]] that powered the force field to the hangar.<ref name="fug" />

Although the generators were scattered around the city, Serra quickly discovered the generators' locations and uploaded the information to the squadron's tactical maps. Two generators were located in the city's southern slums, and the last was in the [[Bantha/Legends|bantha]] pens on the northern side of the city. After eliminating the stormtroopers defending the generators, the Renegades rendered the generators inoperative. With the shield generators disabled, Renegade Squadron was finally able to move inside the hangar. The stormtroopers guarding the prisoners attempted to fend off the Rebels, but after a brief fight, Renegade Squadron was able to defeat them and capture the hangar.<ref name="fug" />

The prisoners were locked in a small cell in the hangar, so Solo started use a [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusioncutter]] to free them. The remainder of the Imperial garrison attempted to retake the Hutt hangar to stop Solo from freeing the slaves, but Renegade Squadron was able to hold back the Imperial assault for a few minutes while Solo finished his work. As soon as the prisoners were freed, the Imperials sent in two [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]] to pin the group in the Hutt hangar. However, the Renegades destroyed the two [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] while the liberated Rebels made their way back to the southern slums where the ''Falcon'' was waiting for them. When all the prisoners were aboard, General Solo prepped the ship for takeoff while Commander Serra and the rest of the Squadron defended the landing site. Once the ship was ready to go, Solo ordered everyone aboard and took off, leaving Tatooine and the remaining Imperials behind.<ref name="fug" />

==Aftermath==
Although the Alliance considered the mission a success and a miracle rescue, it was later learned that the entire mission was a trap. Boba Fett knew the Rebels would attempt a rescue, so he planted a [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracking device]] on an [[Unidentified R5-series astromech droid|R5-series astromech droid]] that had accompanied the Rebels during the mission. Fett tracked the Rebels to their new base on [[Boz Pity/Legends|Boz Pity]], and after a few hours, an Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] over the planet, beginning an [[Battle of Boz Pity (Galactic Civil War)/Legends|assault on the base]].<ref name="fug" />

Years later, after the formation of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Tionne Solusar]] tracked down Col Serra to ask him about Renegade Squadron for her [[Jedi Archives/Legends|archives]]. He would tell her about many of their battles and missions, including their mission to Tatooine.<ref name="fug" />

==Behind the scenes==
The mission made its first and only appearance in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]'', which recounts the various battles and exploits of Renegade Squadron during the Galactic Civil War. The battle itself makes up the fifth level in the game's campaign mode.<ref name="fug" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]''
